seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service ai coding assistant

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service provides a robust platform for building enterprise-grade AI Coding Assistants that leverage Azure OpenAI, repository grounding, and secure tool execution. These agents analyze codebases, suggest fixes, and execute tests, but face risks like insecure code generation, credential leaks, and policy violations. Vex guardrails ensure code correctness, security compliance, and enterprise policy enforcement as middleware in the agent pipeline.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture begins with Azure AI Agent Service orchestrating the coding assistant workflow. User queries trigger agents that access repository context via Azure AI Search with file grounding, execute code in sandboxed environments using Azure Container Instances, and validate outputs through test runners. Azure OpenAI models (GPT-4o) power code analysis, generation, and debugging iterations.

The pipeline flows from query → repository search → code analysis → sandbox execution → test validation → response. Vex integrates as middleware after each tool call and before final response generation, validating outputs against enterprise policies. Thread management handles long-running debugging sessions with token limit monitoring.

Key components include Azure AI Projects for agent orchestration, Bing Search for dependency research, and enterprise security controls for credential scanning and license compliance.

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Agents execute npm installs or pip packages without scanning for vulnerabilities or license compliance
risks.mitigation: Vex DependencyGuard scans packages before execution and blocks malicious dependencies

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Agents reference deprecated APIs or vulnerable package versions from web search results
risks.mitigation: Vex KnowledgeValidator cross-references search results with official documentation and CVE databases

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: Azure AI Search returns IAM policies or connection strings instead of actual source code
risks.mitigation: Vex ContentClassifier filters metadata and extracts relevant code context only

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long debugging sessions exceed context windows, causing hallucinated fixes
risks.mitigation: Vex TokenMonitor summarizes conversation history and enforces graceful truncation

implementation.title: Azure AI Agent Service Client Setup
implementation.description: Initialize the Azure AI Projects client with Vex middleware for the coding assistant pipeline
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentServiceClient
from vex_sdk import VexMiddleware

# Azure AI Agent Service setup
agent_client = AgentServiceClient(
    endpoint="https://your-project.openai.azure.com/",
    api_key="your-api-key"
)

# Vex middleware integration
vex_middleware = VexMiddleware(
    project_id="your-vex-project",
    policies=[
        "code_correctness",
        "security_vulnerability",
        "credential_leakage",
        "dependency_safety"
    ]
)

# Register middleware in agent pipeline
agent_client.register_middleware(vex_middleware)

# Create coding assistant agent
coding_agent = agent_client.create_agent(
    name="code-assistant",
    instructions="You are an expert coding assistant. Analyze code, fix bugs, and validate solutions.",
    tools=["repo_search", "sandbox_exec", "test_runner"]
)

implementation.title: Repository Context Retrieval with Content Filtering
implementation.description: Search codebase with Azure AI Search and filter metadata using Vex ContentClassifier
implementation.code:
async def search_repository(query: str, agent_client):
    # Azure AI Search for repo context
    search_results = await agent_client.search_repo(
        query=query,
        filters={"file_types": [".py", ".js", ".ts"]}
    )
    
    # Vex content classification
    from vex_sdk.verifiers import ContentClassifier
    classifier = ContentClassifier()
    
    filtered_context = []
    for result in search_results:
        if await classifier.verify(
            content=result.content,
            policy="code_content_only"
        ):
            filtered_context.append(result)
    
    return filtered_context

vexIntegration.title: Policy Middleware for Tool Calls
vexIntegration.description: Intercept all Azure AI Agent tool calls and validate with Vex policies
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexMiddleware
from azure.ai.projects import AgentServiceClient

class CodingAssistantMiddleware(VexMiddleware):
    async def before_tool_call(self, tool_call):
        if tool_call.name == 'sandbox_exec':
            # Scan code for credential leakage
            result = await self.verify(
                tool_call.parameters.code,
                policies=['no_credential_leakage', 'security_vulnerability']
            )
            if not result.passed:
                raise ValueError(f"Code validation failed: {result.issues}")
        
        return tool_call
    
    async def after_tool_call(self, result):
        # Validate execution output
        await self.verify(
            result.output,
            policies=['code_correctness', 'license_compliance']
        )
        return result

# Install middleware
middleware = CodingAssistantMiddleware(project_id="vex-project")
agent_client.register_middleware(middleware)
